meaning: n.椹宠埞,娓歌墖vt.鐢ㄨ埞杩愯緭vi.韫掕窔,闂叆
examples: Raw materials were carried to the works by barge.
She wants to cruise the canals of France in a barge.
A barge was close inshore about a hundred yards away.
Brink was seen to barge into Brown, sending the former Scotland manager off balance.
The silt will be removed from the river using an excavator on a barge.
